summary: This speculative fiction piece presents a timeline of fake news articles from 2019-2022 depicting the catastrophic consequences of advances in genetic engineering and CRISPR technology. It begins with a US bioweapon attack on North Korea, escalates through genetic database breaches enabling targeted assassinations, and culminates in a radical environmentalist group engineering human extinction through mass sterilization. The narrative warns that the same tools enabling miraculous cures like fixing cystic fibrosis also enable unprecedented bioweapons. Inspired by a DEFCON 25 talk on genetic hacking, the piece illustrates how quickly society could unravel once biological engineering becomes cheap and accessible.
key_points: CRISPR and genetic engineering could enable both revolutionary cures and devastating bioweapons, and the line between them is razor-thin
Genetic databases held by private companies like 23andMe represent an existential security risk if breached, enabling individually-targeted biological attacks
State actors normalizing bioweapons (the US attacking North Korea) opens a Pandora's box that non-state actors inevitably exploit
The decreasing cost and increasing accessibility of genetic engineering means containment becomes effectively impossible
The fictional timeline compresses plausible developments into just four years, suggesting these risks are nearer than we think
money_quotes: Researchers announced yesterday that they were able to design a disease which corrects the genome of patients suffering from the early stages of cystic fibrosis.
A similarly benign virus could be introduced to the general public to eliminate the disease across the entire human population.
It was specifically designed to target a number of individuals named John Roberts, and all other infected persons were unaffected.
The attackers simply targeted all genomes matching that name.
A highly contagious disease engineered to bring about impotence has infected most of the Earth's population.
Members of isolated communities are asked to avoid contact with the outside world.
Work is underway to determine which members of the population have escaped exposure, and plan for the continuity of the species.
key_insight: The same genetic engineering breakthroughs that promise miraculous cures will inevitably be weaponized, and the combination of cheap CRISPR technology with leaked genetic databases could enable targeted killings and even engineered extinction.
